# Atlas

> Turn any URL or bookmark into AI knowledge — automatically synced to your coding tools.

Atlas is a CLI tool that captures URLs and converts them into AI skills and knowledge notes, then syncs them into your coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf).

## How to set up Atlas for the user

Follow these steps in order. Run each command for the user and confirm it succeeds before moving on.

### Step 1 — Check Node.js version

Atlas requires Node.js 18 or higher.

```bash
node --version
```

If the version is below 18, install the latest LTS from https://nodejs.org before continuing.

---

### Step 2 — Install Atlas globally

```bash
npm install -g atlas-ai
```

Verify the installation:

```bash
atlas --version
```

---

### Step 3 — Check for an AI provider

Atlas needs at least one AI provider to classify and generate content. Check what is available:

```bash
# Check for Claude CLI
which claude

# Check for OpenCode CLI
which opencode

# Check for Anthropic API key
echo $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
```

If none of these are set up, install Claude CLI:

```bash
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
```

Or set an Anthropic API key:

```bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
```

Add it to the shell profile (~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc) so it persists:

```bash
echo 'export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...' >> ~/.zshrc
source ~/.zshrc
```

---

### Step 4 — Run the setup wizard

```bash
atlas init
```

The wizard will walk through three choices:

1. **Browser** — which browser's bookmarks Atlas should watch. If the user only wants to capture manually, choose Skip.
2. **Coding tools** — which AI coding tools to sync entries to. Atlas will auto-detect which ones are installed and pre-select them.
3. **AI provider** — which LLM to use. Atlas will auto-detect available options and pre-select the best one.

After completing the wizard, Atlas creates `~/.ai-knowledge/config.json` with the user's choices.

---

### Step 5 — Capture the first URL

Test that everything is working end to end:

```bash
atlas capture https://react.dev/learn/hooks-overview
```

This will:
- Fetch and extract the page content
- Classify it as a skill or knowledge note using AI
- Generate a structured markdown file
- Sync it to all configured coding tools

---

### Step 6 — Confirm sync worked

```bash
# List all captured entries
atlas list

# Check provider sync status
atlas providers status
```

If a provider shows as not healthy, run:

```bash
atlas providers sync
```

---

## Useful commands once set up

```bash
atlas capture <url>                 # Capture any URL
atlas capture <url> --as skill      # Force it to be saved as a skill
atlas capture <url> --as knowledge  # Force it to be saved as a knowledge note
atlas capture <url> --dry-run       # Preview without saving

atlas list                          # See everything captured
atlas list --type skill             # Skills only
atlas list --tag react              # Filter by tag

atlas search "event loop"           # Search by keyword

atlas show <slug>                   # Read a specific entry

atlas delete <slug>                 # Remove an entry

atlas providers status              # Check sync health
atlas providers sync                # Re-sync everything

atlas daemon start                  # Start the background bookmark watcher
atlas daemon stop                   # Stop the background bookmark watcher
atlas daemon status                 # Check if the daemon is running
```

---

## Optional: Start the bookmark watcher daemon

If the user configured a browser in Step 4, they can enable automatic capture. When running, any URL added to the "Atlas" bookmark folder in their browser is captured automatically — no manual `atlas capture` needed.

```bash
atlas daemon start
```

Verify it is running:

```bash
atlas daemon status
```

To stop it:

```bash
atlas daemon stop
```

The daemon log is at `~/.ai-knowledge/.daemon.log`. If something goes wrong, check that file first.

---

## Troubleshooting

**"No AI provider available"**
Install Claude CLI (`npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`) or set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.

**Provider shows as not detected**
The coding tool is not installed or its config directory does not exist. Install the tool first, then run `atlas providers sync`.

**Capture fails with extraction error**
The site may block automated requests. Try `atlas capture <url> --dry-run` to see what was fetched, or use `--as skill` to skip AI classification and force the type manually.

**Daemon won't start**
Run `atlas init` and ensure a browser is selected (not "Skip"). The daemon needs a browser configured to know which bookmarks file to watch. Also confirm the browser has been opened at least once so its bookmarks file exists.

**Re-run setup at any time**
```bash
atlas init
```
